gloss.en: a chemical element found in all living things

words.example: Diamond is made of carbon.

gloss.en: thick, stiff paper used for making boxes

words.example: We packed the books in a cardboard box.

gloss.en: a knitted sweater that opens at the front

words.example: She wore a warm wool cardigan.

gloss.en: having no worries or problems

words.example: They spent a carefree summer at the beach.

gloss.en: kind and helpful to other people

words.example: She is a warm and caring person.

gloss.en: a public festival with music and dancing

words.example: The whole town joined the street carnival.

gloss.en: a group sharing one car to travel together

words.example: We started a carpool to save money on gas.

gloss.en: a small waterfall, or a flow of something

words.example: Water fell in a beautiful cascade over the rocks.

gloss.en: all the people who act in a play or film

words.example: The whole cast came on stage to bow.

gloss.en: not formal

words.example: He wore casual clothes to the party.

gloss.en: in a relaxed or informal way

words.example: She spoke casually about her new job.

gloss.en: a list of items, products, or books

words.example: I ordered the shoes from a catalog.

gloss.en: a thing that causes change to happen

words.example: The new law was a catalyst for change.

gloss.en: a sudden event causing great damage or suffering

words.example: The flood was a terrible catastrophe.

gloss.en: causing great damage or suffering

words.example: The storm had catastrophic effects on the town.

gloss.en: an act of catching something, like a ball

words.example: He made a great catch in the game.

gloss.en: pleasing and easy to remember

words.example: The song has a catchy tune.

gloss.en: the act of putting things into groups

words.example: The categorisation of books helps readers find them.

gloss.en: the act of putting things into groups

words.example: Good categorization makes the data easy to search.

gloss.en: providing food and drink for events

words.example: They hired a company for the wedding catering.

gloss.en: a large and important church

words.example: We visited the old stone cathedral.

gloss.en: the person or thing that makes something happen

words.example: Unemployment is a major cause of poverty.

gloss.en: a large hole in a hill or under the ground

words.example: They explored a dark cave in the mountain.

gloss.en: the top inside surface of a room

words.example: The room has a high ceiling.

gloss.en: the smallest unit of living matter

words.example: The body is made of red and white blood cells.

gloss.en: a room under a building, often for storage

words.example: He keeps wine in the cellar.

gloss.en: a person who plays the cello

words.example: She is a talented cellist.

gloss.en: a large stringed musical instrument

words.example: He plays the cello in the orchestra.

gloss.en: a temperature scale; Celsius

words.example: Water boils at one hundred degrees centigrade.

gloss.en: the state of being certain

words.example: There is no certainty that the plan will work.

gloss.en: an official document that proves facts are true

words.example: She showed her birth certificate.

gloss.en: official approval that something meets a standard

words.example: The product earned a safety certification.

gloss.en: to state officially that something is true

words.example: A doctor must certify that he is fit to work.

gloss.en: a series of connected metal rings

words.example: She wore a gold chain around her neck.

gloss.en: a seat for one person, with a back and legs

words.example: Please sit on this chair.

gloss.en: the person in charge of a meeting

words.example: The chairman opened the meeting.

gloss.en: a difficult task that tests your ability

words.example: The new job is a big challenge for her.

gloss.en: difficult in an interesting way

words.example: The exam was challenging but fair.

gloss.en: a competition to find the best player or team

words.example: Our team won the championship this year.

gloss.en: a top government or university official

words.example: The chancellor announced the new budget.

gloss.en: in a state of complete confusion and disorder

words.example: The traffic was chaotic this morning.

gloss.en: very typical of someone or something

words.example: This kindness is characteristic of her.

gloss.en: very pleasant or attractive

words.example: The cottage is tiny, but it's charming.

gloss.en: a diagram or table that shows information

words.example: The chart shows our sales each month.

gloss.en: an act of running after someone to catch them

words.example: The thieves were caught after a short chase.

gloss.en: to act dishonestly to gain an advantage

words.example: He tried to cheat in the exam.

gloss.en: a place where people are stopped and checked

words.example: We showed our passports at the checkpoint.

gloss.en: either side of the face below the eyes

words.example: The baby has rosy cheeks.

gloss.en: rude in a playful or amusing way

words.example: The boy gave a cheeky grin.

gloss.en: to shout loudly to show support or joy

words.example: The crowd began to cheer loudly.

gloss.en: in a happy and positive way

words.example: She smiled and waved cheerfully.

gloss.en: the quality of being happy and positive

words.example: Her cheerfulness made everyone feel better.

gloss.en: treatment of disease using chemicals or drugs

words.example: She is having chemotherapy for cancer.

gloss.en: loved and cared for very much

words.example: These photos are my most cherished memories.

gloss.en: a small, round, red or black fruit

words.example: She put a cherry on top of the cake.

gloss.en: to break food with your teeth before swallowing

words.example: You should chew your food slowly.

gloss.en: most important

words.example: Money was the chief reason for the move.

gloss.en: behaving like a child; silly or immature

words.example: Stop being so childish about it.

gloss.en: a pipe that takes smoke out of a building

words.example: Smoke rose from the chimney.

gloss.en: a long thin piece of fried potato

words.example: He ate a burger and chips.

gloss.en: a group of people who sing together

words.example: She sings in the school choir.

gloss.en: to cut something into pieces with a knife

words.example: He was chopping logs for the fire.

gloss.en: a route or path around a place

words.example: The runners did three laps of the circuit.

gloss.en: the system of electric circuits in a device

words.example: The phone's circuitry was damaged by water.

gloss.en: the facts and conditions affecting a situation

words.example: In this circumstance, we had no other choice.

gloss.en: to mention as a reason or example

words.example: She cited several examples to prove her point.

gloss.en: a person who legally belongs to a country

words.example: He became a citizen of the United States.

gloss.en: happening across a whole city

words.example: They held a citywide clean-up event.

gloss.en: connected with the people who live in a country

words.example: The country suffered years of civil war.

gloss.en: a developed and organized human society

words.example: Ancient Egypt was a great civilization.

gloss.en: a sea creature with a shell in two parts

words.example: We ate fresh clam soup by the sea.

gloss.en: to hit your hands together to show you like it

words.example: The audience began to clap loudly.

gloss.en: to make something easier to understand

words.example: Could you clarify what you mean?

gloss.en: accepted as one of the best of its kind

words.example: It is a classic film that everyone loves.

gloss.en: to arrange things into groups by shared features

words.example: The books are classified by subject.

gloss.en: a group of words with a subject and a verb

words.example: This sentence has two clauses.

gloss.en: the quality of being easy to see or understand

words.example: I admired the clearness of her writing.

gloss.en: relating to office work

words.example: She does clerical work in the office.

gloss.en: a person who serves customers in a shop

words.example: The clerk gave me the wrong change.

gloss.en: a person who uses a professional's services

words.example: The lawyer met with her client.

gloss.en: a high rock with a steep side, often by the sea

words.example: We stood at the edge of the cliff.

gloss.en: a place for special medical treatment or advice

words.example: She went to the clinic for a check-up.

gloss.en: a short part of a film shown separately

words.example: Here is a clip from her latest movie.

gloss.en: to block something so nothing can pass

words.example: Leaves can clog the drain.

gloss.en: near in space or time

words.example: My house is close to the station.

gloss.en: in a near or careful way

words.example: He watched the game closely.

gloss.en: clothes, especially of a particular type

words.example: Workers wear protective clothing.

gloss.en: moving in an awkward way and dropping things

words.example: He is so clumsy that he broke the cup.

gloss.en: training or teaching someone a skill

words.example: She gives tennis coaching to children.

gloss.en: rough in texture; not smooth or fine

words.example: The sand felt coarse under my feet.

gloss.en: the outline of a coast where land meets sea

words.example: The coastline stretched for miles.

gloss.en: a brown powder used to make chocolate

words.example: She made a warm cup of cocoa.

gloss.en: a large brown tropical nut with white flesh

words.example: We drank fresh coconut water on the beach.

gloss.en: the quality of being clear and logical

words.example: His essay lacked coherence.

gloss.en: clear, logical, and easy to understand

words.example: She gave a clear and coherent answer.

gloss.en: two things happening together by chance

words.example: It was a strange coincidence that we met.

gloss.en: a sudden failure of something

words.example: The collapse of the bank shocked everyone.

gloss.en: a person you work with

words.example: I had lunch with a colleague.

gloss.en: a person who collects things as a hobby or job

words.example: He is a stamp collector.

gloss.en: words that are often used together

words.example: "Strong tea" is a common collocation.

gloss.en: the punctuation mark ( : )

words.example: Use a colon before a list.

gloss.en: an area governed by another, more powerful country

words.example: India was once a British colony.

gloss.en: full of bright or many colours

words.example: The garden was full of colourful flowers.

gloss.en: a deep unconscious state after injury or illness

words.example: He was in a coma for two weeks.

gloss.en: two or more things joined into one unit

words.example: The dish is a combination of sweet and sour.

gloss.en: the state of being relaxed and free from pain

words.example: The hotel offers a high level of comfort.

gloss.en: in a relaxed and pleasant way

words.example: She sat comfortably in the soft chair.

gloss.en: funny and making you laugh

words.example: He told a comic story at dinner.

gloss.en: an order given to a person or animal

words.example: The dog obeyed every command.

gloss.en: an officer in charge of soldiers or an operation

words.example: The commander gave the order to advance.

gloss.en: to honour the memory of a person or event

words.example: The statue commemorates the fallen soldiers.

gloss.en: an official group given responsibility for something

words.example: The commission will report its findings soon.

gloss.en: a promise to do or support something

words.example: Marriage is a long-term commitment.

gloss.en: a group chosen to make decisions or handle a topic

words.example: The committee met to discuss the plan.

gloss.en: usually; very often; by most people

words.example: This name is commonly used in France.

gloss.en: a group of countries or a nation as a whole

words.example: Many nations joined the commonwealth.

gloss.en: willing and able to talk and share information

words.example: She is friendly and communicative.

gloss.en: all the people living in one area as a group

words.example: The local community supported us.

gloss.en: to travel regularly between home and work

words.example: I commute to work by train.

gloss.en: measured by comparing with something else

words.example: They now live in comparative comfort.
